steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The graph of $f$ has x-intercepts at $-6$, $0$, $4$, and $7$. A quadratic factor of $f$ must have roots chosen from that same set of x-intercepts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x^2 - 2x - 24$: factors as $(x-6)(x+4)$, roots $6$ and $-4$. Neither is in $\{-6, 0, 4, 7\}$. ✗
  • Choice B — $x^2 - x + 42$: discriminant $= 1 - 168 \lt 0$, no real roots, so it cannot be a factor of a polynomial with four real zeros. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^2 - 11x - 28$: roots $\approx 13.1$ and $\approx -2.1$, neither in the zero set. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^2 - 7x$: factors as $x(x-7)$, roots $0$ and $7$. Both are zeros of $f$. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
